exercise_name: Plate Internally Rotated Rear Delt Fly
target_muscle: Traps (mid-back), Shoulders, Posterior Deltoid, Rear Shoulders
instructions:
1. With plates in either hand, bend your knees with your feet slightly bowed out. Hinge forward with a flat back.
2. Maintain an overhand grip and internally rotated shoulders.
3. Raise your arms backward toward the ceiling while maintaining a light band in your elbows.

1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip two plates in each hand at your sides.
2. Keeping your arms straight, raise the plates out to your sides until they are in line with your shoulders.
3. Lower the plates back to your sides with control. Repeat for desired number of reps.

1. Start by standing upright with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a single weight plate overhead with both hands, keeping your arms extended straight above your head.
2. Begin walking forward, taking slow and steady steps while maintaining a straight line. Your focus should be on maintaining balance and control of the weight plate overhead.
3. As you walk, continue to keep your arms extended and the weight plate overhead. Try to maintain good posture and avoid leaning forward or backward, as this could result in losing control of the weight plate.
4. Continue walking for a set distance or for a certain amount of time, keeping your focus on proper form and technique. When finished, return to the starting position and repeat as desired.

1. Stand tall with a weight plate overhead.
2. Elevated and depress your scapula.
3. Avoid leaning forward or backwards to keep pressure off of your lumbar spine.

1. Stand tall with a plate overhead.
2. Step with one foot forward bringing your back knee to the floor. Gently tap your knee then drive through your front leg.
3. Go back to a standing position and then step forward with the opposing legs.

1. Stand tall holding two plates in each hand with a pinch grip.
2. Walk for the desired distance or time.

1. Keep your back straight and bend forward slightly at the waist.
2. With arms extended, raise the weights out to your sides until they are in line with your shoulders.
3. Pause briefly and then lower the weights back to the starting position.

1. Start by sitting on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground
2. Engage your core muscles by pulling your belly button toward your spine.
3. Slowly twist your upper body to the right, keeping your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
4. Hold for a second and then twist to the left, holding for another second.

1. Start by lying on your back on a mat with your legs bent and feet flat on the ground.
2. Hold a weight plate over your head with both hands, making sure to keep your arms straight.
3. Engage your core and use your abdominal muscles to sit up, keeping the weight plate towards the ceiling.

1. Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, with a weight plate held in both hands at chest height.
2. Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your weight in your heels.
3. Go down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then pause for a moment.
4. Hold the squat position for the desired amount of time, keeping the weight plate steady and your core engaged.

1. Get into a kneeling position with both forearms pressed into the ground.
2. Place a weight plate on your back (or have a partner do it for you).
3. Hold the plank for the allotted amount of time.

1. Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a weight plate.
2. Place one foot slightly behind the other, with the heel of your back foot elevated.
3. From this starting position, bend at the hips and knees to lower the plate towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
4. Once the plate is below your knees, drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.

1. Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding two plates with a pinch grip.
2. Place one foot slightly behind the other, with the heel of your back foot elevated.
3. From this starting position, bend at the hips and knees to lower the plates towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.

target_muscle: Glutes, Shoulders, Anterior Deltoid, Quads, Front Shoulders
instructions:
1. Start with the plate at chest height. Begin a squat.
2. Make sure to hit parallel depth. On your way back up, use the momentum you generate with your legs to help push the plate into an overhead position.
3. Let the plate return to chest height before initiating the next squat.

1. Start in a standing position with a plate against your chest.
2. Press the plate away from your body until your elbows are extended.

1. Stand with a plate extended away from your body. Elbows mostly extended.
2. Rotate at the upper spine while keeping your lumbar spine in a neutral position.
3. Do not let the plate go behind your body.

1. Start in a kneeling position with your hands planted on the ground.
2. Place a weight plate on your back or have a friend do it for you.
3. Pick your knees up off the ground. Hold in this position with a flat back.

1. Lay flat on the ground with your arms extended over head holding a weight plate.
2. Pick your upper back and your legs off the ground. Your body should be in a banana shape.

1. Stand with a shoulder width stance. Begin with the plate near one of your quads.
2. Rotate at your upper spine to engage the obliques.
3. You can pivot your back foot to keep from generating too much torque at the knee.

exercise_name: Barbell Feet Elevated Figure Four Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the barbell placed over your hips, and cross one leg over the other.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the barbell placed over your hips.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

exercise_name: Barbell Feet Elevated Single Leg Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the barbell placed over your hips. Raise one leg up and off the box.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

exercise_name: Barbell Feet Elevated Staggered Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the barbell placed over your hips. Place one foot about 6-9 inches in front of the other.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

exercise_name: Bodyweight Feet Elevated Figure Four Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying on your back with your knees bent and feet on top of a sturdy box, bench or elevated surface.
2. Drive through your heels to lift your hips off the floor, making sure to keep your feet and knees pointing forward.

1. Start by lying on your back with your knees bent and feet on top of a sturdy box, bench or elevated surface.
2. Drive through your heels to lift your hips off the floor, making sure to keep your feet and knees pointing forward.

exercise_name: Bodyweight Feet Elevated Single Leg Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying on your back with your knees bent and feet on top of a sturdy box, bench or elevated surface. Lift one leg off the box.
2. Drive through your heel to lift your hips off the floor, making sure to keep your feet and knees pointing forward.

exercise_name: Bodyweight Feet Elevated Staggered Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying on your back with your knees bent and feet in a staggered position on the box.
2. Drive through your heels to lift your hips off the floor, making sure to keep your feet and knees pointing forward.

1. Stand with feet staggered and one hand on a bench/box. Hold a dumbbell in the opposing hand.
2. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping the back flat,  and eyes facing forward. The weight should hang directly below the shoulders.
3. Start with an overhand grip, as you pull rotate the dumbbell. When your upper arm reaches your side your palm should be facing forward.

1. Sit on a flat bench with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
2. Lean forward and place your elbow on the inside of your leg.
3. Curl the dumbbell while keeping your elbow against your leg. Make sure to fully extend your elbow on each rep.

1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet flat on the floor and your knees bent.
2. Hold a dumbbell over your clavicle.
3. Engage your core muscles and lift your torso off the floor until your shoulders are a few inches off the ground.
4. Lower yourself back down to the starting position, but avoid resting completely on the floor between reps.

1. Start by placing two dumbbells on the floor, about shoulder-width apart.
2. Position yourself in a plank position with your hands on top of the dumbbells, keeping your arms straight.
3. Keep your feet together, with your toes resting on the floor.
4. Engage your core and lower your body towards the floor by bending your arms, until your chest nearly touches the dumbbells.
5. Push your body back up to the starting position, straightening your arms and engaging your chest, triceps and shoulders.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Feet Elevated Figure Four Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the dumbbell placed over your hips, and cross one leg over the other.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

1. Start by lying on your back with your knees bent and feet on top of a sturdy box, bench or elevated surface. Place a dumbbell over your hips.
2. Drive through your heels to lift your hips off the floor, making sure to keep your feet and knees pointing forward.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Feet Elevated Single Leg Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the dumbbell placed over your hips. Raise one leg up and off the box.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Feet Elevated Staggered Glute Bridge
instructions:
1. Start by lying on your back with your knees bent and feet in a staggered position on the box.
2. Drive through your heels to lift your hips off the floor, making sure to keep your feet and knees pointing forward.

1. Stand with feet staggered and one hand on your knee. Hold a dumbbell in the opposing hand.
2. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping the back flat, and eyes facing forward. The weight should hang directly below the shoulders.
3. Start with an overhand grip, as you pull rotate the dumbbell. When your upper arm reaches your side your palm should be facing forward.

1. Lay on your side on a bench or box. Place one hand on the floor and the other hand is holding the dumbbell.
2. While keeping your elbow mostly extended, raise the dumbbell to the side.
3. The end of the range of motion is when the dumbbell is pointed toward the ceiling.
4. Make sure you don't flex and extend your elbow.

1. Start by sitting on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground. Hold a dumbbell in both hands with your arms extended straight in front of you.
2. Twist your torso to the right, bringing the dumbbell towards the ground, then return to the center. Repeat on the left side, twisting to the left and bringing the dumbbell towards the ground.
3. Continue this twisting motion, alternating sides, for the desired number of repetitions. Keep your core engaged and avoid swinging your arms to generate momentum.

secondary_muscles: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and hold two dumbbells overhead with both hands, using an overhand grip.
2. Begin the squat by bending at the hips and knees, keeping your core tight and the dumbbells balanced overhead.
3. Lower your body as far as you can while keeping the dumbbells overhead, then push back up to the starting position.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, making sure to keep your back straight, chest lifted, and the dumbbells stable overhead throughout the entire movement.

secondary_muscles: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Posterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders, Rear Shoulders
instructions:
1. Start in a plank position. Hold one dumbbell.
2. Raise the dumbbell straight out in front of you (I), at a 45 degree angle (Y), out to the side (T), and then bend at the elbow while raising (W).
3. Going through IYTW counts as one rep.
4. Make sure to keep your hips stable. If you're unable to, try performing this exercise from the knees.

1. Start in a high plank position with a pair of dumbbells positioned under your shoulders. Your hands should grip the dumbbells, palms facing each other.
2. Keep your core engaged and your back straight as you row one dumbbell up towards your hip, keeping your elbow close to your body.
3. Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position and repeat on the other side.

1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and place your right hand on the right hip.
2. Hold a dumbbell with the left hand. Bend at the hips to the side.
3. Make sure you don't start to lean forward to try and force a longer range of motion.

secondary_muscles: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and a dumbbell in your right hand, extending it overhead.
2. Inhale and begin to squat down, keeping the dumbbell overhead and your back straight.
3. Go down as far as you can while still maintaining control, then exhale and return to standing position, keeping the dumbbell overhead. Repeat for desired reps and then switch to the other arm.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Single Arm Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions:
1. Stand tall with a single dumbbell extended overhead.
2. Flex at the elbow letting the dumbbell travel behind your body.
3. Try not to let your elbow flare outward.

1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
2. Keep your back straight and chest lifted, engage your core to maintain balance.
3. Begin the movement by lifting the dumbbell straight up towards your chin, keeping your elbow close to your body and the weight in line with your shoulder.
4. Continue to lift the weight until it reaches the chin or neck level, keeping the elbow high and close to the body.
5. Lower the weight back down to the starting position with control.

1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding 2 dumbbells in each hand.
2. Keep your back straight and chest lifted, engage your core to maintain balance.
3. Begin the movement by lifting the dumbbells straight up towards your chin, keeping your elbow close to your body and the weight in line with your shoulder.
4. Continue to lift the weight until it reaches the chin or neck level, keeping the elbow high and close to the body.

1. Stand with a dumbbell held on the side of your leg.
2. Raise that leg laterally as high as your hip range of motion allows for.

1. Lay flat on the ground with a dumbbell. Point the soles of your shoes at the ceiling as well as the dumbbell.
2. Reach for your feet with the dumbbell before bringing your spine back to the floor.

1. Lay flat on the ground on your stomach with your arms extended overhead. Hold a dumbbell with a neutral grip.
2. Lift your legs and chest off the ground simultaneously and hold for a 1 count at the top.
3. Relax your lower back and lower your legs and chest back to the floor.

1. Grab both dumbbells and hinge forward at the hips. Make sure you keep a flat back.
2. The closer your torso is to parallel with the ground the longer the range of motion will be at your shoulder. The better the results you'll get from the exercise.
3. Let your arms hang freely, maintain an underhand grip, and then pull your elbow joint straight back toward the ceiling.

1. Lay flat on a bench with two dumbbells extended toward the ceiling.
2. Maintain an overhand grip. Flex at the elbows.
3. The dumbbells will land on your upper chest/clavicle.
4. Extend your elbows bringing the dumbbells back into their starting position.
5. *Named after Dave Tate*

1. Grasp the TRX handles with both hands, keeping your arms extended and your shoulders away from your ears.
2. Keep your abs engaged, roll forward onto your toes, and extend your arms in front of you.
3. Slowly lower your body down to the ground, keeping your arms straight and your abs engaged.
4. When your head gets between your arms, pause for a moment and then use your abs to pull your body back up to the starting position.

1. Hold the TRX straps under your arms and the handles in your armpits.
2. Lean forward against the straps.
3. Keep your body straight and initiate the calf raise by raising your heels off the ground.

1. Start by adjusting the TRX straps to chest height. Stand facing the TRX with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Grasp the TRX handles with both hands, keeping your arms extended and your shoulders away from your ears.
3. Lean forward, keeping your arms extended and your core engaged. Your body should form a slight diagonal line from your ankles to your head.
4. Slowly bring your arms towards each other, in front of your chest, while keeping your core engaged and your body in a stable position.

1. Hold the TRX straps with an underhand grip and lean back and away from the anchor point.
2. Keep your elbows high as you flex your elbows. Pull your fists toward your forehead.

1. Stand facing away from the anchor point, holding onto the TRX handles with both hands.
2. Step your left foot behind your right, crossing it behind your right leg, and lower yourself into a curtsy lunge.
3. Push through your right foot to return to the starting position and repeat the movement with the other leg.

1. Lie face down on the ground and place your forearms on the ground directly under your shoulders.
2. With your feet in the stirrups, kick both feet into the TRX straps and hold the handles above your head.
3. Tighten your abs, glutes, and legs to form a straight line from your head to your feet.

1. Lie on your side with your legs straight and your feet resting in the TRX foot cradles.
2. Prop yourself up on your forearm and engage your core to maintain a straight line from your head to your feet.

1. Lie on your back with your feet in the foot cradles of the TRX straps, with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
2. Place your arms by your sides with your palms facing down.
3. Drive your hips up towards the ceiling, squeezing your glutes and hamstrings at the top of the movement.

1. Grab the TRX straps and lean away from the anchor point.
2. Maintain a neutral grip. Flex your elbows and pull your fists to either sides of your temples.

1. Start by facing the TRX straps and positioning yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the ground and your feet in the TRX foot cradles.
2. Keeping your core engaged and body in a straight line, bend your knees and bring your heels towards your glutes.
3. Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.

1. Start by facing away from the TRX straps and placing your feet in the TRX handles with your palms on the ground.
2. Lift your knees off the ground to begin the set.
3. Engage your core and maintain this position for the desired amount of time, holding a steady plank.

1. Stand facing the TRX straps and hold onto the handles with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Keeping your core engaged, slowly raise your arms forward until they are overhead.
3. Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.

1. Stand facing the TRX straps and place your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Begin by lowering into a squat position by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
3. Explosively jump up off the ground, using your arms for momentum if desired.
4. Land softly back in the squat position, making sure to absorb the impact with your legs.

1. Begin in a kneeling position with the TRX straps adjusted to mid-calf length.
2. Grasp the TRX handles with both hands, keeping your arms extended and your shoulders away from your ears.
3. Keep your abs engaged, roll forward onto your toes, and extend your arms in front of you.
4. Slowly lower your body down to the ground, keeping your arms straight and your abs engaged.

1. Place your feet in the TRX stirrups and get in to a hand plank position.
2. Pull your knees to your chest and flex your abs.

1. Grasp the TRX handles with both hands and extend your arms straight in front of you.
2. Step laterally to one side, keeping your arms extended and your core engaged.
3. Lower your body into a lunge position, keeping your front knee bent at a 90-degree angle and your back leg straight.
4. Push off your front foot to return to the starting position, keeping your arms extended and your core engaged.

1. Start in a plank position with the TRX straps adjusted to mid-calf length. Place your feet in the TRX stirrups.
2. Keep your abs engaged, and bring one knee towards your chest while keeping the other leg extended behind you.
3. Switch legs, bringing the opposite knee towards your chest while keeping your abs engaged and your back straight.

1. Place your feet in the TRX stirrups and get in to a hand plank position.
2. Pull both knees to your right elbow.
3. Extend your legs back to the starting position, then pull both knees to the left elbow.

1. Place your feet in the stirrups and get in to a plank position.
2. Pull your hips straight up toward the ceiling.
3. Lower your hips until you're back in a plank position.

1. Start by setting up the TRX straps to a secure anchor point and adjust the straps so that they hang about waist height when you stand under them.
2. Stand facing the TRX straps, grab the handles with both hands, and step forward so that your body is in a plank position with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.
3. Lower your body down by bending your elbows until your chest nearly touches the handles. Keep your core tight and your body in a straight line.
4. Push your body back up to the starting position, straightening your arms as you do so. Make sure to engage your chest, triceps, and core throughout the movement.

1. Stand facing away from the TRX straps and grab the handles with both hands.
2. Step forward so that your body is in a plank position, with your arms straight and your body in a straight line from your head to your heels.
3. Keeping your arms straight, slowly lift your arms out to the side, keeping them at the same height as your shoulders. Your body should form a "T" shape.

1. Stand facing away from the TRX straps and grab the handles with both hands.
2. Step away so your body is leaning away from the anchor point.
3. Flex your elbows and pull your elbows straight back behind your body.
4. Make sure to keep your elbows flared outward.

1. Stand facing the TRX straps and grasp the handles with both hands, palms facing away from your body.
2. Walk back until the straps are taut, keeping your arms straight.
3. Slowly curl your hands towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.

1. Hold on to the TRX straps and step away until the strap is taut.
2. Step backwards with one leg in to a lunge position and gently tap your knee to the floor.
3. Lunge back to the starting position and repeat with the other leg.

1. Grab the TRX straps and lean away from the anchor point until the strap is taut.
2. Start with an overhand grip. Pull your elbows back and rotate your grip.
3. By the time your elbows reach your side, your palms should be facing upwards.

1. Hold the TRX strap with one hand.
2. Lean back until the strap is taut. Fully extend your elbow.
3. Pull your elbow behind the body. Make sure to avoid twisting. Try to keep your shoulders even.

1. Hold the two TRX straps and lean forward. The straps should be over each shoulder.
2. Keep your elbows tucked in and then flex and extend at the elbows.

1. Hold the two TRX straps and step away until the straps are taut. Take a split stance.
2. Bring your back knee to the floor and gently tap the floor.
3. Use your arms and the TRX to help assist you getting back into the starting position.

1. Hold the two TRX straps and lean away from the anchor point.
2. Flex at the hips and knees in to a squat position.
3. You can use your arms and the straps to assist you back into the starting position.

1. Hold the TRX straps and lean away from the anchor point.
2. Pull your elbows back while externally rotating your shoulder joint. Your arms should make a W shape at the back portion of the exercise.

target_muscle: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders
instructions:
1. Hold the TRX straps and lean away from the anchor point until the straps are taut.
2. Keep both elbows mostly extended and raise both arms out and upwards.
3. You should make a Y shape with your arms during the back portion of the exercise.

1. Begin in a tabletop position with your hands and knees on the ground.
2. Sit back onto your heels and extend your arms out in front of you.
3. Rest your forehead on the ground.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then release.

exercise_name: Child Pose Arms Extended Left Right
instructions:
1. Begin in a tabletop position with your hands and knees on the ground.
2. Sit back onto your heels and extend your arms out in front of you to one side.
3. Rest your forehead on the ground.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then repeat on the other side.

1. Begin in a tabletop position with your hands and knees on the ground.
2. Sit back onto your heels and place your arms at your sides with your palms facing up.
3. Rest your forehead on the ground.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then release.

1. Start in a tabletop position on your hands and knees with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
2. Turn your hands so that your fingers are pointing towards the sides of your mat and the tops of your hands are on the floor.
3. Keep your arms straight and your shoulders away from your ears.
4. Hold the pose for several breaths or as long as is comfortable.

1. Start in a tabletop position on your hands and knees with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
2. Turn your hands so that your fingers are pointing towards your knees and the tops of your hands are on the floor.
3. Keep your arms straight and your shoulders away from your ears.
4. Hold the pose for several breaths or as long as is comfortable.

1. Come onto your hands and knees on the mat with your wrists directly under your shoulders and your knees under your hips.
2. Turn your palms down and spread your fingers wide, with your fingertips pointing towards your knees.
3. Press into your palms and extend your arms fully to lift your shoulders away from your ears.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, and then release by slowly lowering your hands to the mat and coming back to a neutral tabletop position.

1. Lie on your stomach with your arms at your sides.
2. Bend your knees and bring your heels towards your buttocks.
3. Reach back with your hands and grab your ankles.
4. Inhale and lift your chest off the ground, pulling your legs up and back with your hands.
5. Keep your gaze forward and hold the pose for a few breaths before releasing back to the ground.

1. Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your arms at your sides.
2. Cross your right arm over your left arm, bringing your palms together in front of your chest.
3. Lower your arms down so that your palms face down towards the ground, and your right elbow is resting on top of your left elbow.
4. Hold for a few breaths, then switch sides.

1. Begin on your hands and knees with your wrists directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
2. Inhale and arch your back, lifting your tailbone and head towards the ceiling, while lowering your belly towards the floor.
3. Exhale and round your spine, tucking your chin towards your chest, and bringing your tailbone towards your knees.
4. Repeat these movements, flowing smoothly between the two poses, for several breaths.

1. Stand straight with your feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Inhale and raise your arms straight up over your head.
3. Exhale and bend your knees, lowering your hips as if you were sitting in a chair.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, then inhale and stand up straight.

1. Place two blocks on the floor, shoulder-width apart.
2. Kneel in front of the blocks, and lower your torso down so that your elbows are resting on the blocks, and your forehead is resting on the floor.
3. Relax your arms, shoulders, and neck, and breathe deeply.
4. Hold for a few breaths, then release.

1. Lie down on your back with your legs straight and arms at your sides.
2. Close your eyes and take deep breaths in through your nose and out through your mouth.
3. Allow your body to completely relax and let go of any tension.
4. Hold for several minutes and then slowly come back up to a seated position.

1. Begin in Runner's Lunge pose with your right foot forward.
2. Slowly lift your torso up and bring your arms overhead, palms facing each other.
3. Make sure your right knee is directly above your ankle, and your left leg is straight behind you.
4. Hold for a few breaths before repeating on the other side.

1. Begin in Crescent Moon Pose with your right foot forward and a block nearby.
2. Place the block at the highest height under your left hand.
3. Reach your left arm behind you and take hold of your left foot or ankle.
4. Gently pull your foot towards your buttocks while pressing your left hand into the block to deepen the stretch in your left quad. Hold for a few breaths before releasing and repeating on the other side.

exercise_name: Crescent Moon Pose Quad Stretch With Block
instructions:
1. Begin in Crescent Moon Pose with your right foot forward and a block nearby.
2. Place the block at the highest height under your left hand.
3. Reach your left arm behind you and take hold of your left foot or ankle.
4. Gently pull your foot towards your buttocks while pressing your left hand into the block to deepen the stretch in your left quad. Hold for a few breaths before releasing and repeating on the other side.

1. Begin in a squatting position with your feet together and your knees apart.
2. Place your hands on the floor in front of you, shoulder-width apart. Keep your arms straight and engage your core.
3. Slowly shift your weight forward and lift your feet off the ground, coming onto your tiptoes.
4. Bend your elbows and lean your weight onto your hands, shifting your knees towards your upper arms.
5. Gently lift one foot off the ground, and then the other, balancing on your hands. Hold the pose for a few breaths before releasing back to the ground.

1. Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Place a block under your left knee, then bend your right knee and stack it on top of your left ankle.
3. Flex both feet and gently press your knees towards the ground, feeling a stretch in your hips.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, then switch sides and repeat.

1. Begin on your hands and knees with your hands shoulder-width apart and your knees hip-width apart.
2. Spread your fingers wide and press down into your palms, making sure that your fingers are evenly spaced and facing forward.
3. Tuck your toes under and lift your knees off the ground, straightening your legs as much as possible.
4. Press your hips up and back, bringing your body into an inverted "V" shape.
5. Keep your arms straight and your shoulder blades spread apart, and try to press your heels down towards the ground.
6. Hold the pose for several breaths, then release and return to your starting position.

1. Begin in a downward-facing dog position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your feet hip-width apart.
2. Lift your right foot off the mat and place your right toes on top of your left heel.
3. Press your palms and fingers firmly into the mat and lift your hips up towards the ceiling, deepening the stretch in your hamstrings and calves.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, and then release by slowly walking your hands back out to a downward-facing dog position.

exercise_name: Downward Dog With Fingers Facing Feet
instructions:
1. Begin in the same starting position as the previous exercise, with your hands and knees on the ground.
2. Spread your fingers wide and turn your hands so that your fingers are facing towards your feet.
3. Tuck your toes under and lift your knees off the ground, straightening your legs as much as possible.
4. Press your hips up and back, bringing your body into an inverted "V" shape.
5. Keep your arms straight and your shoulder blades spread apart, and try to press your heels down towards the ground.
6. Hold the pose for several breaths, then release and return to your starting position.

1. Sit cross-legged on the floor and bring your arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
2. Cross your right arm over your left, bending both elbows so that your forearms are parallel to the floor.
3. Lift your elbows towards the ceiling and bring your hands together, then drop your chin towards your chest.
4. Hold for a few breaths, then release your arms and repeat on the other side.

1. Begin seated or standing with your arms at your sides.
2. Inhale and reach your arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
3. Cross your right arm over your left arm, bringing your palms to touch if possible.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then release the arms and repeat with the left arm over the right.

1. Begin by sitting cross-legged on the floor.
2. Slowly drop your chin towards your chest, feeling a stretch in the back of your neck.
3. Inhale and lift your head up, then exhale and drop your right ear towards your right shoulder.
4. Hold for a few breaths, then switch sides and repeat the stretch.

1. Begin seated with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Bend your right knee and place your foot on the floor next to your left thigh.
3. Inhale and reach your left arm up towards the ceiling.
4. Exhale and twist to the right, placing your left elbow on the outside of your right knee. Hold for a few breaths and then repeat on the other side.
5. Begin seated with your legs extended in front of you.
6. Bend your right knee and place your foot on the floor next to your left thigh.
7. Inhale and reach your left arm up towards the ceiling.
8. Exhale and twist to the right, placing your left elbow on the outside of your right knee. Hold for a few breaths and then repeat on the other side.

1. Begin in a lunge position with your right foot forward and your left foot back.
2. Place your right hand on a block or the ground on the outside of your right foot.
3. Reach your left arm overhead and then round your spine, bringing your left hand to your right ear.
4. Look down towards your right foot as you reach your left arm towards the front of the room, feeling a stretch in your side body. Hold for a few breaths and then switch sides.

exercise_name: Extended side Angle Pose With Block
instructions:
1. Begin in a lunge position with your right foot forward and your left foot back.
2. Place a block on the outside of your right foot and place your right hand on top of it.
3. Look down towards your right foot as you reach your left arm towards the front of the room, feeling a stretch in your side body. Hold for a few breaths and then switch sides.

exercise_name: Gate Pose Variation Arm Extended on Side
instructions:
1. Start on your knees and extend your right leg out to the side.
2. Raise your right arm above your head and reach towards the left side of the room.
3. Gently bend your body to the left side and rest your left hand on your left thigh.
4. Look up towards your right hand and hold the pose for a few breaths before switching sides.

exercise_name: Gate Pose Rounding Spine Looking Up
instructions:
1. Begin in a kneeling position with your knees hip-width apart.
2. Extend your right leg out to the side, placing your foot flat on the ground.
3. Reach your right arm overhead and then round your spine, bringing your right hand to your left ear.
4. Look down towards your left foot as you reach your left arm towards your right foot, feeling a stretch in your side body. Hold for a few breaths and then switch sides.

1. Sit on a yoga mat with legs straight in front of you.
2. Bend the right knee and place the right foot on top of the left thigh. Allow the right knee to fall towards the floor.
3. Bring the left foot onto the right thigh. Keep the spine straight and rest hands on the knees.
4. Hold for several breaths and then release.

1. Start in a tabletop position with your hands and knees on the floor.
2. Step your right foot forward, placing it between your hands.
3. Straighten your right leg and flex your toes towards your face, keeping your hands on the floor for support.
4. Hold for several breaths, then switch sides and repeat.

1. Sit cross-legged on the floor and interlace your fingers behind your head.
2. Drop your chin to your chest and roll your head to the right, bringing your right ear towards your right shoulder.
3. Slowly roll your head back to center, then over to the left so that your left ear is close to your left shoulder.
4. Repeat the movement from side to side, keeping your shoulders relaxed and breathing deeply.

1. Lie down on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Lift your feet off the floor and bring your knees towards your chest.
3. Reach inside your knees and hold onto the outside edges of your feet.
4. Gently pull your knees towards your armpits and rock side to side for a few breaths.

1. Begin on your hands and knees with your wrists under your shoulders and your knees under your hips.
2. Place your forearms on the floor and interlace your fingers.
3. Place the crown of your head on the floor and tuck your toes under.
4. Walk your feet towards your head and lift your hips up.
5. Straighten your legs and find your balance. Hold for several breaths and then release.

1. Begin by standing tall with your feet hip-width apart.
2. Shift your weight onto your left foot and bring your right heel towards your glute.
3. Reach your right arm back and grab onto the inside of your right foot or ankle.
4. Inhale and lift your right foot away from your glute while extending your left arm forward. Hold for 5-10 breaths before switching sides.

1. Begin in a low lunge with your right foot forward and your left knee on the ground.
2. Slowly slide your left knee back while extending your right leg forward, keeping both legs straight.
3. Lower your body down to the ground, using your hands for support if needed.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, then switch sides and repeat.

1. Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and your arms at your sides.
2. Press your feet into the ground and engage your leg muscles.
3. Lift your chest and roll your shoulders back and down.
4. Take a deep breath in, and as you exhale, release any tension in your body and relax your muscles.

1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Bend your left knee and place your left foot on the floor, close to your right hip.
3. Cross your right leg over your left leg, stacking your knees on top of each other.
4. Bring your left arm up overhead, and bend your elbow so that your left hand reaches towards your upper back. Reach your right hand behind your back and try to clasp your left hand. Hold

1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Inhale and lengthen your spine, then exhale and fold forward from your hips, keeping your spine long.
3. Reach for your feet or ankles, keeping your elbows straight.
4. Hold for several breaths, then release.

1. Extend your right leg behind you and place your right ankle on top of your left thigh, with your right knee pointing out to the side.
2. Sitting up straight, use your hands to gently press down on your right knee while at the same time pulling your left buttock back.
3. Hold for 5-10 deep breaths, then release and repeat on the other side.

1. Start standing at the top of your mat with your feet wide apart.
2. Interlace your fingers behind your back and inhale to expand your chest.
3. Exhale and fold forward from your hips, bringing your hands up and over your head towards the ground.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then release the hands and come back up to standing.

1. Begin in a tabletop position with your hands and knees on the ground.
2. Walk your hands forward as you lower your chest towards the ground.
3. Keep your hips over your knees and extend your arms out in front of you.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then release.

1. Begin in Tadasana (Mountain Pose) with two blocks at the top of your mat.
2. Step your left foot back about 3-4 feet and place your heel down, keeping your toes pointing forward.
3. Place the blocks on either side of your right foot at a height that allows you to comfortably place your hands on them.
4. Inhale and lengthen your spine, then exhale and fold forward from your hips, bringing your hands to the blocks. Hold for several breaths, then switch sides and repeat.

1. Start standing with a flat and vertical block on the matt
2. Bend forward, placing hand on vertical block, and front of foot on the flat block.
3. Press the bottom of your foot towards the ground, and repeat on each side.

1. Begin in Tadasana (Mountain Pose).
2. Step your left foot back about 3-4 feet and place your heel down, keeping your toes pointing forward.
3. Inhale and lengthen your spine, then exhale and fold forward from your hips, bringing your hands to the floor on either side of your right foot.
4. Hold for several breaths, then switch sides and repeat.

1. Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place your hands on a table, chair, or any elevated surface in front of you.
2. Walk your feet back until your torso is parallel to the ground, and your elbows are resting on the surface.
3. Relax your head and neck, and breathe deeply.
4. Hold for a few breaths, then release.

1. Lie down on your back with your knees bent and your feet on the ground.
2. Cross your right ankle over your left knee, creating a figure four shape.
3. Use your hands to gently pull your left knee towards your chest, feeling a stretch in your right hip.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, then switch sides and repeat.

1. Start in a seated position with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Cross your right leg over your left leg, placing your right foot on the floor beside your left hip.
3. Cross your left leg over your right leg, placing your left foot on the floor beside your right hip.
4. Relax your arms by your sides or place them on your abdomen and take deep breaths.

target_muscle: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders
instructions:
1. Start seated or standing with your arms at your sides.
2. Bring your hands behind your back and interlace your fingers with your palms facing up.
3. Inhale and draw your shoulder blades together, lifting your chest.
4. Exhale and gently press your hands down towards the floor as you continue to lift your chest.

1. Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Bend your right knee and bring your heel towards your perineum.
3. Place your left hand on the outside of your right knee and twist your body to the right.
4. Reach your right hand towards your left foot and look up towards the ceiling. Hold the pose for a few breaths before switching sides.

exercise_name: Revolved Side Angle Pose With Prayer Hands Beginner
instructions:
1. Begin in a low lunge position with your right foot forward and your left knee on the ground.
2. Bring your hands together in a prayer position at your heart.
3. Exhale and twist your torso to the right, placing your left elbow on the outside of your right knee.
4. Hold for several breaths and then repeat on the other side.

exercise_name: Revolved Side Angle Pose With Prayer Hands
instructions:
1. Begin in Warrior II pose, with your right foot forward and your left foot back.
2. Bring your hands together in a prayer position at your heart.
3. Exhale and twist your torso to the right, placing your left elbow on the outside of your right knee.
4. Hold for several breaths and then repeat on the other side.

1. Begin in Downward Facing Dog pose (Adho Mukha Svanasana).
2. Step your right foot forward between your hands.
3. Place your left knee on the ground behind you and slide it back until you feel a stretch in your left hip flexor.
4. Keep your hands on the ground or lift them to the sky and hold for a few breaths before repeating on the other side.

1. Begin in a downward-facing dog pose.
2. Slowly lift one leg up towards the ceiling, keeping your hips level and your toes pointed.
3. Bend your lifted leg and try to touch your toes to the back of your head.
4. Once you have a firm grip with your toes, slowly begin to twist your torso towards the lifted leg.
5. Keep your hands and feet firmly planted on the ground as you hold the pose for a few breaths. Release the pose and repeat on the other side.

1. Sit cross-legged on the floor.
2. Extend your arms straight out in front of you, palms facing forward.
3. Cross your right arm over your left arm and interlace your fingers.
4. Pull your hands away from your body and hold for a few breaths, then release and repeat with the opposite arm on top.

1. Sit comfortably on the floor with your legs crossed.
2. Inhale and raise your right arm above your head.
3. As you exhale, bend your body to the left side and rest your left hand on the ground.
4. Look up towards the ceiling and hold the pose for a few breaths. Then switch sides and repeat the pose on the other side.

1. Begin seated with your legs extended in front of you.
2. Bend your right knee and place your foot on the floor next to your left thigh.
3. Inhale and reach your left arm up towards the ceiling.
4. Exhale and twist to the right, placing your left elbow on the outside of your right knee. Hold for a few breaths and then repeat on the other side.

1. Start by lying down on your stomach on a yoga mat or a comfortable surface.
2. Place your elbows underneath your shoulders, keeping your forearms parallel to each other.
3. Press your palms and forearms firmly into the ground to lift your chest up, while keeping your hips and legs relaxed.
4. Make sure your shoulders are relaxed and away from your ears. Gently roll your shoulder blades down and back to open up your chest.
5. Lengthen your spine by lifting the crown of your head towards the ceiling, while keeping your chin tucked in slightly.

1. Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place a block on the ground in front of you.
2. Hinge forward at your hips and place your hands on the block.
3. Allow your head to hang heavy and feel a stretch in the back of your legs.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then slowly roll up to a standing position.

1. Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
2. Hinge forward at your hips, reaching your hands towards the ground.
3. Allow your head to hang heavy and feel a stretch in the back of your legs.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then slowly roll up to a standing position.

1. Lie down on your back with your arms stretched out to your sides.
2. Bend your right knee and bring it towards your chest.
3. Slowly twist your body to the left side, bringing your right knee across your body towards the ground on the left side.
4. Look towards your right hand and hold the pose for a few breaths before switching sides.

1. Sit on the floor with your legs stretched out in front of you.
2. Place a yoga block behind you, horizontally, at the base of your spine.
3. Slowly recline over the block, supporting yourself with your forearms, until your back is resting on the block.
4. Relax your arms at your sides, palms facing up, and breathe deeply.

exercise_name: Thread The Needle Hip Over Knees
instructions:
1. Start on your hands and knees in a tabletop position.
2. Slide your right knee up between your hands and place your right foot close to your left wrist.
3. Take your left leg back and lower it down to the ground, keeping your toes tucked.
4. Twist your torso to the right and reach your right arm underneath your left arm, threading the needle. Hold for a few breaths, then switch sides.

1. Start in mountain pose, standing with your feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Shift your weight onto your left foot and bend your right knee, bringing the sole of your right foot to the inside of your left thigh.
3. Bring your hands together in front of your heart, or raise them overhead.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, then release and repeat on the other side.

1. Begin in a comfortable seated position on your mat, with your legs crossed and your spine straight.
2. Extend your right arm up toward the ceiling and bend it at the elbow, bringing your right hand behind your head.
3. Reach your left arm behind your back and bend it at the elbow, bringing your left hand up between your shoulder blades.
4. Clasp your hands behind your back, trying to touch your fingertips together. If you can't quite reach, you can use a strap or towel to bridge the gap between your hands.

1. Lie down on your back with your feet hip-distance apart and your knees bent.
2. Place your hands on the floor beside your head, fingertips pointing towards your shoulders.
3. Push your feet and hands into the floor, lift your hips up and straighten your arms.
4. Lift your chest towards the ceiling and hold the pose for a few breaths before lowering back down.

1. Begin in a kneeling position with your knees together and your shins and feet on the floor.
2. Sit back on your heels and place your palms face down on your thighs, with your fingers pointing towards your knees.
3. Relax your shoulders and take deep breaths.
4. Hold the pose for several breaths or as long as is comfortable.

1. Begin in a kneeling position with your knees together and your shins and feet on the floor.
2. Sit back on your heels and place the tops of your hands on your thighs, with your fingers pointing towards your knees.
3. Relax your shoulders and take deep breaths.
4. Hold the pose for several breaths or as long as is comfortable.

1. Begin in a downward-facing dog position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your feet hip-width apart.
2. Walk your feet towards your hands, bending your knees as needed to allow your heels to touch the mat.
3. Press your palms and fingers into the mat and lift your hips up towards the ceiling to deepen the stretch in your hamstrings and calves.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, and then release by slowly walking your hands back out to a downward-facing dog position.

1. Start in mountain pose, standing with your feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Step your left foot back about 3-4 feet and turn it slightly inward, while keeping your right foot facing forward.
3. Inhale and raise your arms overhead, then exhale and bend your right knee, bringing it directly over your ankle.
4. Hold the pose for a few breaths, then inhale and return to mountain pose. Repeat on the other side.

1. Start in mountain pose, standing with your feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Step your left foot back about 3-4 feet and raise it off the ground, while keeping your right foot facing forward.
3. Inhale and reach your arms forward, parallel to the ground.
4. Exhale and hinge forward at your hips, bringing your torso parallel to the ground and extending your left leg straight back. Keep your arms and right leg straight and hold the pose for a few breaths
5. inhale and return to mountain pose. Repeat on the other side.

1. Start in mountain pose, standing with your feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Step your left foot back about 3-4 feet and turn it slightly inward, while keeping your right foot facing forward.
3. Inhale and raise your arms to shoulder height, parallel to the ground, with your palms facing down.
4. Exhale and bend your right knee, bringing it directly over your ankle while keeping your left leg straight.
5. Look over your right hand and hold the pose for a few breaths, then inhale and return to mountain pose. Repeat on the other side.

exercise_name: Wide Legged Standing Forward Bend Holding Heels
instructions:
1. Start standing at the top of your mat with your feet wide apart.
2. Start standing at the top of your mat with your feet wide apart.
3. Exhale and fold forward from your hips, bringing your hands up and over your head towards the ground.
4. Hold for a few breaths and then release the hands and come back up to standing.

1. Start in a downward facing dog position.
2. Shift your weight onto your left hand and foot, and lift your right leg towards the ceiling.
3. Slowly bend your right knee and shift your weight onto your right hand as you open up your chest towards the ceiling.
4. Look towards your right hand and hold the pose for a few breaths before switching sides.

1. Stand facing a wall with your arms extended and your palms pressing into the wall at shoulder height.
2. Slowly begin to walk your feet away from the wall, keeping your arms straight and your hands pressing into the wall.
3. Walk until your arms are fully extended and your body is at a 45-degree angle to the ground.
4. Repeat From High to Low Angles starting above the shoulder, and ending below the chest.

1. Stand in front of a box.
2. *Start with a low box if this is your first time*
3. Sink into a quarter of half squat and then pop your hips forward.
4. Try to land in the middle of the box as softly as possible to minimise impact on your joints.

1. Stand on top of a box. Step off of the box and land on the floor.
2. Land as softly as possible. Squat as you land to minimise impact on your joints.
3. Go straight into another hop after you've landed. This will help you learn to absorb impact.

1. Setup two boxes. One to jump on and one to sit.
2. Go from a seated position and pop your hips forward forcefully.
3. Jump and land on the box softly.

1. Stand with a shoulder width stance.
2. Jump onto your box and land with one foot.
3. Try to land in the middle of the box and land as softly as possible.

1. Stand in front of a low box.
2. Bring one foot to the edge of the box and alternate as quickly as possible.
3. Only lightly tap your feet to the box.

1. Position yourself on a glute ham raise machine or find a suitable substitute like a stability ball or bench.
2. Adjust the equipment to fit your body by setting the footplate at an appropriate height and making sure your feet are securely anchored.
3. Begin the movement by slowly lowering your upper body towards the ground while keeping your torso and legs aligned.
4. Once you reach the lowest point where your body is parallel to the ground or as far as you can comfortably go, pause briefly.
5. Engage your glutes and hamstrings again as you push yourself back up to the starting position, using the strength of your posterior chain muscles.

exercise_name: Medicine Ball Chest Press Partner Toss
secondary_muscles: Triceps, Long Head Tricep, Lateral Head Triceps
instructions:
1. Stand tall, partner tosses medicine ball to chest.
2. Catch ball and press it back to partner. Repeat.

secondary_muscles: Triceps, Long Head Tricep, Lateral Head Triceps, Medial Head Triceps
instructions:
1. Hold medicine ball at chest, feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Extend arms forward, slamming ball to the ground.
3. Catch the ball on the rebound and repeat for reps.

1. Stand in front of a wall holding a medicine ball.
2. Press the ball forward, extending arms and tossing it.
3. Catch the ball and return to the starting position.

exercise_name: Medicine Ball Clean And Press Slam
instructions:
1. Hold the medicine ball with an overhand grip.
2. Lift the ball explosively, clean it to shoulder height.
3. Press the ball overhead, then slam it to the ground.

exercise_name: Medicine Ball Half Kneeling Wood Chopper
instructions:
1. Kneel with one knee down, holding a medicine ball.
2. Rotate torso and until the medicine ball is pointed the opposite direction.
3. Repeat the movement on the other side.

target_muscle: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Posterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders, Rear Shoulders
instructions:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hold a medicine ball.
2. Circle the ball around your head, maintaining control.
3. Complete the desired number of repetitions in both directions.

target_muscle: Glutes, Quads, Inner Quadriceps, Outer Quadricep
instructions:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding medicine ball close.
2. Squat down, keeping ball close to head and chest.
3. Stand back up, squeezing glutes. Repeat for desired reps.

1. Stand tall with a medicine ball held against one hip.
2. Lift the leg on the opposite side laterally.
3. Lower the leg and repeat on the other side.

1. Kneel, holding a medicine ball.
2. Rotate torso and slam the ball diagonally down and across.
3. Repeat the movement on the other side.

1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball.
2. Step laterally, performing a lunge, then slam the ball.
3. Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.

1. Hold medicine ball at one shoulder, feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Squat down, keeping ball on same side throughout movement.
3. Stand back up, squeezing glutes. Repeat on other side.

1. Hold medicine ball overhead.
2. Step back into lunge position.
3. Return to starting position. Repeat on other leg.

1. Hold medicine ball overhead.
2. Squat down, keeping back straight.
3. Stand back up. Repeat for desired reps.

1. Stand facing partner, side by side.
2. Toss medicine ball to partner's side.
3. Catch and repeat toss on other side.

1. Lie facing partner, knees bent.
2. Sit up, toss medicine ball to partner.
3. Catch and lower down. Repeat for desired reps.

1. Place forearms on medicine ball, extend legs.
2. Maintain straight body position for desired time.

1. Hold medicine ball at chest.
2. Jump, extending arms overhead.
3. Return to starting position. Repeat for desired reps.

target_muscle: Triceps, Chest, Mid and Lower Chest
instructions:
1. Place hands on medicine ball, in push-up position.
2. Perform push-up, maintaining proper form.

1. Lift medicine ball overhead.
2. Slam ball down to one side.
3. Catch and repeat slam to the other side.

target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Quads, Inner Quadriceps, Outer Quadricep
instructions:
1. Hold medicine ball at chest.
2. Step back into lunge position.
3. Return to starting position. Repeat on other leg.

target_muscle: Glutes, Quads, Inner Quadriceps, Outer Quadricep
instructions:
1. Hold medicine ball at chest.
2. Step back into lunge position, extend your arms, twist torso.
3. Return to starting position. Repeat on other leg.

1. Sit with knees bent, feet off ground.
2. Twist torso side to side, touching medicine ball to ground.

1. Toss medicine ball up, catch with both hands.
2. Repeat toss and catch for desired reps.

1. Hold medicine ball while standing on one leg.
2. Hinge forward on one leg, extending other leg behind.
3. Return to starting position. Repeat on other leg.

1. Lift medicine ball overhead.
2. Slam ball down to ground with force.

1. Hold medicine ball at chest.
2. Squat down, reach ball to one side.
3. Stand up and repeat reach on other side.

1. Place one hand on medicine ball, other on ground.
2. Perform push-up, walking hands to switch positions.

1. Hold medicine ball at chest, facing wall.
2. Squat down, then throw ball at wall.
3. Catch and repeat for desired reps.

1. Hold medicine ball with both hands and arms extended.
2. Swing ball diagonally across body, engaging core.

1. Stand sideways next to a wall.
2. Swing medicine ball across your body and toss off the wall.
3. Catch and repeat toss on the other side.

1. Set up the landmine attachment and load weight.
2. Stand facing the landmine, holding the weight away from your chest.
3. Step backward with one leg, lunge down while twisting your torso toward the front leg, return to starting position, and repeat on the other side.

exercise_name: Landmine Alternating Lunge To Chest Press
instructions:
1. Stand facing the landmine with feet hip-width apart and holding the end of the landmine barbell with both hands, keeping elbows bent and close to your body.
2. Step backward with the right foot, lunge down, and press the landmine away from your chest.
3. Push through the left heel to return to the starting position while pulling the landmine back to your chest.
4. Repeat on the opposite side, alternating for the desired number of reps.

target_muscle: Shoulders, Lateral Deltoid, Anterior Deltoid, Front Shoulders
instructions:
1. Stand facing the landmine leaning forward slightly with the end of the barbell held in one hand at your shoulder with your elbow positioned beneath your wrist.
2. While keeping your elbow pointed forward, drive the bar directly overhead, switching hands at the top of the movement. Lower the bar back down to the other side of your body, mirroring the starting position.
3. Repeat for desired number of reps, switching hands at the top of each rep.

1. Stand beside the landmine with your feet shoulder-width apart while grasping the end of the landmine barbell with the hand farther away from the landmine attachment, palm facing up and your elbow close to your side.
2. Curl the barbell up towards your opposite shoulder, contracting your bicep muscles, then slowly lower the barbell back down.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Stand facing the barbell, holding the end of the barbell close to your chest in both hands with your elbows tucked in to your sides.
2. Drive though the balls of your feet to raise your heels off the ground, squeezing your calves, with a focus on making sure your ankles do not turn out or in.
3. Lower your heels back down to the ground and repeat for the desired number of reps.

1. Start on one knee facing the landmine, holding the end of the barbell with your hand on the side of your forward leg.
2. While keeping your elbow braced against the inside of your knee, curl the barbell upward towards your shoulder, then lower it back down with control.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Stand facing the barbell while holding the end of the bar at your side with one hand.
2. With the leg on the same side of your body as the barbell, lunge backward and across your body at a 45 degree angle while keeping your weight on your front heel to lower your back knee gently to the ground.
3. Drive through your front heel as you step back to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Start on your hands and knees perpendicular to the barbell with the end of the barbell resting on the inside of your knee that is closer to the bar.
2. Keeping your core braced, your back flat, and your moving leg bent at a 90 degree angle, drive the heel of your working leg up the ceiling while squeezing your glute.
3. Follow the movement path as you bring your knee back down to the ground beneath your hip, keeping your knee bent to maintain control of the barbell.
4. Repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Start facing the barbell while holding the end of the bar in the goblet position in front of your chest with your elbows close to your sides.
2. Step backward and across your body with one leg at a 45 degree angle while keeping your weight on your front heel to gently lower your back knee to the ground.
3. Drive through your front heel as you step back up and to the starting position.
4. Repeat for the desired number of reps before switching legs.

1. Start facing the barbell while holding the end of the bar close to your chest in the goblet position with your elbows tucked in close to your body.
2. Step directly out to the side with both toes pointing forward. Keep your weight on the heel of the leg you step out with as you sink down over that foot while your other leg remains straight for balance.
3. Drive through the heel over which you have centered your body as you step back to the starting position.
4. Repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Start facing away from the landmine attachment with the end of the barbell resting on one shoulder against the base of your neck while leaning slightly back into the weight of the barbell.
2. Squat down with control while keeping the weight of the barbell as close to centered over your midpoint as possible, then drive through your heels to stand all the way back up and return to starting position.
3. Repeat for desired number of reps, then switch the side of your body the barbell is on every subsequent set.

exercise_name: Landmine Half Kneeling Single Arm Overhead Press
instructions:
1. Start facing the landmine on one knee with your other foot in front of you while holding the barbell in one hand at your shoulder on the same side as the knee that is on the ground.
2. Drive the bar upwards while keeping your elbow pointed forward until your arm holding the bar is fully extending, then control the bar as your lower it back to the starting position.
3. Repeat for desired number of reps, then switch sides.

1. Start by lying flat on your back with your legs extended towards the landmine attachment and your arms overhead, holding the landmine barbell with both hands while engaging your core muscles by pressing your lower back into the ground and drawing your belly button in towards your spine.
2. Lift your legs off the ground, keeping them together and straight, and simultaneously raise your upper body off the ground, reaching the landmine barbell away from your face.
3. Hold this position for the desired duration, then carefully lower the barbell to the ground.

1. Start in a kneeling position facing the barbell with one hand holding the end of the barbell at your shoulder with your elbow tucked beneath your wrist.
2. Keeping your elbow pointing forward, drive the barbell up overhead until your arm is fully extended and switch the bar to your other hand at the top of the movement.
3. Control the descent of the bar to your other shoulder and repeat movement for desired reps, switching hands at the top of each rep.

1. Start kneeling in front of the landmine, facing towards it, with your knees hip-width apart and your back straight while holding the end of the bar in both hands in front of your chest.
2. Push the landmine up overhead by fully extending your arms until the weight is directly over your head. Pause briefly at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the landmine back down to the starting position.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps.

1. Kneel down facing the landmine with your knees hip-width apart and your back straight. Grasp the landmine barbell with both hands, interlacing your fingers and holding it above and slightly in front of your body.
2. Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, twist your torso to one side, rotating your arms and the landmine along with it. Pause briefly at the end of the twist, feeling the contraction in your oblique muscles.
3. Slowly rotate back to the starting position, then twist to the opposite side, repeating the same motion.
4. Repeat both sides of the rotation for the desired number of reps.

1. Stand facing the landmine with your feet shoulder width apart while holding the end of the barbell in your hands with your arms fully extended down towards the ground.
2. While keeping your torso tall and core engaged, step directly out to one side, using the foot you have stepped out with to sit down into the lunge while keeping your other leg straight.
3. Drive through your heel on your bent leg as your step together to return to the starting position.
4. Repeat for desired number of reps on the same side before switching sides.

1. Stand perpendicularly to the barbell, holding the end of the bar at your outer hip with your inner hand and your palm facing your body.
2. While keeping your core braced and your arm straight, lift the barbell diagonally up and towards the direction of the landmine attachment, then return the bar to the starting position.
3. Repeat for desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Stand in front of the landmine with your feet hip-width apart and your back straight. Grasp the landmine barbell with one hand, holding it at at your side with your palm facing in towards your leg.
2. Take a step backward with your foot on the same side that you're holding the barbell, lowering your body into a lunge position. Bend both knees to lower your body down until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, or as low as your flexibility allows. Keep your torso upright, your chest lifted, and your core engaged throughout the movement.
3. Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position. Repeat for desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Stand facing the landmine attachment while holding the end of the bar with one hand at your shoulder on the same side with your elbow tucked to your body underneath your wrist.
2. Step back with the leg on the same side that you are holding the bar on and with control lower yourself down into a lunge position while keeping your core engaged and torso tall, then drive through your front heel as you step back to the starting position.
3. Once your feet are back in the starting position, drive the bar up overhead until your arm is fully extended, then lower the bar back down to the starting position.
4. Repeated for the desired number of reps, then switch sides.

1. Start lying on your back perpendicularly to the barbell, holding the bar directly over your shoulder with the hand that's closer to the bar with your palm facing down towards your feet.
2. While keeping your shoulder steady, bend your elbow and lower the end of the bar back towards your the ground next to your head. Keep your hinging elbow pointed down towards your feet.
3. Drive through your palm to extend your elbow and return the bar to the starting position, then repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Stand facing the landmine, with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent. Grasp the landmine barbell with both hands, interlacing your fingers and holding it at chest height. Position your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
2. While keeping your core engaged, rotate your torso and the landmine to one side. Twist through your waist, using your oblique muscles to generate the movement. Pivot on the ball of your opposite foot as you rotate away from it.
3. Rotate back to the starting position, then perform the same movement in the opposite direction.
4. Repeat for the desired number of reps.

1. Stand facing the landmine, holding the end of the bar in both hands with your palms facing each other.
2. Extend your arms upward, pressing the end of the bar up overhead. Pause at the top of the movement, then lower the bar with control back to the starting position.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps.

target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, Lateral Hamstrings
instructions:
1. Stand in front of the landmine with your feet hip-width apart and your knees slightly bent. Grasp the landmine barbell with both hands, palms facing each other, and hold it in front of your thighs.
2. Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and push your hips back, allowing the landmine to travel down your thighs. Lower the landmine by bending forward at the hips while keeping your back flat, allowing the barbell to move closer to the floor. Continue the movement until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your legs relatively straight but with a slight bend in the knees.
3. Reverse the movement by pushing your hips forward and driving through your heels while keeping your back straight.
4. Repeat for the desired number of reps.

1. Stand perpendicularly to the barbell with the end of the bar in front of your feet. Squat down and hold the end of the bar with your hand closer to the landmine attachment with your palm facing your body.
2. Drive your feet into the ground to start the motion. As your legs fully extend, pull the bar upwards with your hand and start to rotate your torso in towards the landmine attachment. As you do so, bring your other hand up to the bar and switch your grip. Press through the bar to extend your arm all the way upright, pivoting your back foot to allow for more trunk rotation.
3. Perform the movement in reverse to return to the starting position. Repeat for desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Start sitting on the ground facing the landmine attachment, holding the end of the bar in both hands slightly in front of and above your chest while holding your feet off the ground and knees bent.
2. Bending your elbows as little as possible and keeping your core tight and feet elevated, lower the end of the bar with control to the ground on one side of your body, then contract your obliques to return the bar to the center. Repeat on the other side.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps.

1. Start sitting on the ground facing the landmine attachment with your legs open for stability while holding the end of the bar in a neutral grip with your hand resting at your shoulder and your elbow beneath your wrist.
2. Leaning forward slightly, press the bar up into the air until your arm is fully extended. At the top of the movement, switch which hand is holding the end of the bar and lower the bar back down to the opposite shoulder. Perform again to return the bar to the starting position.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps.

1. Sit on the ground facing the landmine with your legs spread. Hold the end of the bar in both hands in front of your chest with your palms facing each other.
2. While keeping your torso tall and core brace, drive the end of the bar upward until your arms are fully extended. Lean forward slightly for full range of motion in the shoulder joints. Lower the bar back down to the starting position.
3. Repeat for desired number of reps.

exercise_name: Landmine Seated Single Arm Overhead Press
instructions:
1. Sit on the ground facing the landmine with your legs spread. Hold the end of the bar in one hand in front of that shoulder with your palm facing across your body.
2. While keeping your torso tall and your core braced, drive the bar upwards until your arm is fully extended, leaning forward slightly for full range of motion in the shoulder joint. Lower the bar back down to the starting position with control.
3. Repeat for desired number of reps on one side, then switch sides.

1. Starting lying on your back perpendicularly to the barbell while holding the end of the bar in your hand that is further away from the landmine attachment. Your elbow should be rest on the ground at a 45 to 60 degree angle from your torso, with your wrist stacked over your elbow.
2. Drive the end of the bar upwards, squeezing your chest at the top of the movement. Lower the bar back down until your elbow gently touches the ground.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps on one side before switching sides.

1. Stand in front of the landmine with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent. Grasp the landmine barbell with one hand, palm facing in, and bring it to shoulder height with your elbow tucked beneath your hand.
2. Press the barbell overhead by extending your arm fully, pushing the weight upward. Keep your core engaged, your back straight, and your shoulder down throughout the movement. Pause briefly at the top of the movement with your arm fully extended overhead. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position at shoulder height with control.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps on one side before switching sides.

1. Stand in front of the landmine with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent. Grasp the landmine barbell with one hand, palm facing in, and bring it to shoulder height with your elbow tucked beneath your hand.
2. Dip your body slightly then drive up through the ground to generate momentum as your extend your arm fully upwards. Pause briefly at the top of the rep, then lower the weight back down to the starting position with control.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps on one side before switching sides.

exercise_name: Landmine Single Arm Staggered Overhead Press
instructions:

2. Drive the bar upwards until your arm is fully extended. Pause briefly before lowering the end of the bar back down to the starting position with control.
3. Repeat for the desired number of reps before switching sides.

1. Adjust weight for assistance. Stand on platform, grip handles above shoulder-width. Knees on pad if available.
2. Slowly pull up until chin is above the bar. Keep elbows close, core engaged.
3. Lower back with control. Pause at bottom. Repeat for reps.

1. Adjust weight for assistance. Stand on platform, grip handles above shoulder-width. Knees on pad if available.
2. Slowly pull up until chin is above the bar. Keep elbows close, core engaged.
3. Lower back with control. Pause at bottom. Repeat for reps.

1. Adjust weight for assistance. Stand on platform, grip handles above shoulder-width. Knees on pad if available.

3. Lower back with control. Pause at bottom. Repeat for reps.

1. Adjust weight for assistance. Stand on platform, grip handles above shoulder-width. Knees on pad if available.
