1. Adjust the machine for your height, sit with your back against the pad, knees bent, feet flat on the platform.
2. Brace your core, push through your heels, and extend your hips upwards, lifting the weight while squeezing your glutes.
3. Lower the weight slowly back to the starting position, ensuring controlled movement; repeat for desired reps and sets.

1. Place your feet at the bottom the platform at shoulder width. Lift your heels up.
2. Press against the platform and extend your legs fully, without locking your knees.
3. Lower the weight until your legs are at a 90° angle (but DO NOT allow your butt and lower back to rise off of the pad. This will put your lower back in a rounded position, which is very dangerous.)
4. Raise the weight back to starting position.

exercise_name: Machine Horizontal Leg Press Calf Jump
instructions:
1. Start with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
2. Shift your weight onto the balls of your feet and raise stay flat footed. Using your calf muscles, mimic a jumping motion but don't leave the platform.
3. Explode onto your toes and hold for a second.

exercise_name: Machine Horizontal Leg Press Calf Raise
instructions:
1. Adjust the machine in accordance with your height.
2. The balls of your feet should be supporting your weight on the bottom of the platform, your heels extending off of it.
3. Extend your heels upwards while keeping your knees stationary, and pause at the contracted position.
4. Slowly return to the starting position. Repeat.

1. Place your legs on the platform with your feet at shoulder width.
2. Release the weight and extend your legs fully, without locking your knees.
3. Lower the weight until your legs are at a 90° angle (but DO NOT allow your butt and lower back to rise off of the pad. This will put your lower back in a rounded position, which is very dangerous.)
4. Raise the weight back to starting position.

1. Sit in the machine with your legs apart, pads against your outer thighs. Adjust the seat and pad position comfortably.
2. Slowly squeeze your legs together, focusing on using your inner thigh muscles. Pause when the pads are close together.
3. Slowly return to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid letting the weights slam. Repeat for desired reps and sets.

1. Adjust machine settings to fit your height. Sit with back flat against pad, legs together.
2. Slowly press legs apart against resistance. Pause at peak.
3. Slowly return legs to starting position. Repeat for desired reps.

1. Sit on machine and press your chest into the pad.
2. Grab the handles with an overhand grip. Keep your elbows mostly extended.
3. Bring your arms backwards and squeeze your scapula together.

1. Adjust seat height so handles align with chest. Sit and grip handles with elbows slightly bent.
2. Slowly bring handles together in front, keeping tension. Focus on squeezing chest muscles.
3. Return handles to starting position with controlled motion. Keep chest up throughout. Repeat for reps.

1. Adjust the seat to match your height.
2. Press your back into the back pad.
3. Press upwards until your elbows are nearly extended. But not fully extended.
4. Lower until the plates nearly touch back down.

1. Adjust the seat to match your height.
2. Press your back into the back pad.
3. Press upwards until your elbows are nearly extended. But not fully extended.
4. Lower until the plates nearly touch back down.

1. Stand on the platform and set the pad just over the hip of the working leg.
2. Place the working leg over the pad.
3. Press your leg inward. Aim to get the longest range of motion possible.

2. Place the working leg over the pad.
3. Press your leg outward. Aim to get the longest range of motion possible.

3. Press until your elbows are not extended but not hyperextended.

1. Lie on your back, heels on a stability ball, legs straight, arms by your sides.
2. Press through your heels, lift hips, engage glutes and core, forming a straight line.
3. Slowly lower hips, maintaining control, then repeat for reps.

1. Lie on your back, heels on a stability ball, arms flat, and core engaged.
2. Lift hips, curl the ball toward you using your hamstrings, keeping control and balance.
3. Extend legs back to start while keeping hips lifted, then repeat for desired reps.

1. Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a ball.
2. Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the bar.
3. Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

1. Sit on the floor, place upper back on a stability ball, feet flat, knees bent.
2. Drive hips upward by squeezing glutes, forming a straight line from shoulders to knees.
3. Lower hips slowly without touching the floor, maintaining core engagement, then repeat.

1. Lie sideways on a stability ball, feet anchored, hands behind head, core engaged.
2. Crunch sideways by contracting obliques, bringing your upper body toward your hip.

1. Place feet on a stability ball, hands on the floor in a push-up position.
2. Lower your chest toward the floor, then push up while tucking knees toward your chest.
3. Extend legs back to starting position, maintaining control and stability throughout the movement.

1. Place your hands firmly on the ground, directly under shoulders. Place your feet on the stability ball.
2. Flatten your back so your entire body is straight and slowly lower your body
3. Draw shoulder blades back and down, keeping elbows tucked close to your body
4. Exhale as you push back to the starting position.

1. Place your feet on the ball and get in to a plank position.
2. Pull your hips straight up toward the ceiling.
3. Lower your hips until you're back in a plank position.

1. Start in a plank position with your feet on a stability ball and hands on the floor.
2. Engage your core and tuck your knees toward your chest, rolling the ball forward carefully.
3. Extend your legs back to the starting position, maintaining balance and control throughout the movement.

1. Lie face down on a stability ball, hands on the floor, legs extended straight.
2. Engage glutes and lower back, raising legs until aligned with your torso.
3. Slowly lower legs back down without touching the ground, maintaining core engagement. Repeat.

1. Kneel behind a stability ball, place forearms on top, and engage your core muscles.
2. Lift knees, balance on toes, and slowly move forearms in small controlled circles.
3. Maintain a stable torso, avoid excessive movement, and reverse direction after several rotations.

1. Start by lying flat on your back on a ball with your feet flat on the floor and your knees bent.
2. Engage your core muscles and flex your spine until you feel your abs contract.

1. Lie face-down on a stability ball, hips centered, feet anchored, hands behind your head.
2. Engage your core, lift your upper body until aligned with legs, keeping your neck neutral.
3. Lower your torso slowly back to the starting position, maintaining control and steady breathing.

1. Place a stability ball against a wall at about hip height.
2. Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
3. Break at the hips and knees until you hit parallel or just below, then squat back up.

1. Place a stability ball against a wall at about hip height.
2. Hold two dumbbells in each hand.
3. Break at the hips and knees until you hit parallel or just below, then squat back up.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Front Rack Stability Ball Wall Squat
instructions:
1. Place a stability ball against a wall at about hip height.
2. Hold two dumbbells in front rack position.
3. Break at the hips and knees until you hit parallel or just below, then squat back up.

1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, placing a stability ball between your lower back and wall.
2. Slowly squat down by bending knees to 90 degrees, keeping back straight and core engaged.
3. Press through heels, straighten legs, and return to standing while maintaining balance and control.

target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, Lateral Hamstrings
instructions:
1. Place one knee on a bench and hold a dumbbell.
2. Push your hips back while leaving your knee mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Half Kneeling Goblet Romanian Deadlift
target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, Lateral Hamstrings
instructions:
1. Place one knee on a bench and hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
2. Push your hips back while leaving your knee mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

1. Place one knee on a bench and hold a kettlebell.
2. Push your hips back while leaving your knee mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

exercise_name: Kettlebell Half Kneeling Goblet Romanian Deadlift
instructions:
1. Place one knee on a bench and hold a kettlebell in a goblet position.
2. Push your hips back while leaving your knee mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

1. Place one knee on a bench.
2. Push your hips back while leaving your knee mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

1. Grab the bar shoulder width apart with a supinated grip (palms facing you)
2. With your body hanging and arms fully extended, pull yourself up and lean backwards. Try to touch your chest to the bar.
3. Slowly return to starting position. Repeat.

2. With your body hanging and arms fully extended, pull yourself up until your chin is past the rings.
3. Slowly return to starting position. Repeat.

2. With your body hanging and arms fully extended, pull yourself up and rotate your fists until your chin is past the rings.
3. Slowly return to starting position. Repeat.

2. Pull your body up until your chin is above the rings.
3. Lower your body back to starting position.

exercise_name: Band High To Low Pallof Press Wood Chopper
instructions:
1. Stand sideways to band anchored high, hold handle at chest with both hands.
2. Press arms straight forward, then rotate torso pulling band diagonally downward.
3. Return to center, bending elbows back to chest under control.

exercise_name: Band Pallof Press To Wood Chopper
instructions:
1. Stand sideways to band, hold handle at chest, and brace your core.
2. Press arms straight forward, resisting rotation.
3. Rotate torso and pull band across body in a wood chopper motion, then return.

1. Stand with band anchored low on one side, holding handle at hip.
2. Lean torso away from anchor, keeping hips and legs still.
3. Contract obliques to return upright, maintaining smooth control.

1. Stand with feet hip-width, grip barbell just outside thighs.
2. Explosively extend hips and pull bar to shoulders in one motion.
3. Press overhead until arms lock out, then lower bar under control.

1. Hold bar just above knees with slight bend in hips and knees.
2. Explosively pull bar to shoulders, catching it in a partial squat.
3. Stand tall and press bar overhead until arms are fully extended.

1. Use a bar attachment and set the cable all the way to the top.
2. Walk a few steps forward and fall to a kneeling position.
3. Push your hips back and flex your abs, then push hips forward until in the starting position.

exercise_name: Cable Braced Single Arm Chest Fly
instructions:
1. Stand sideways to cable, hinge forward, and hold handle at shoulder height.
2. Brace free hand against support, then bring working arm across chest in an arc.
3. Squeeze chest at the end, then slowly return to starting position.

exercise_name: Cable Braced Single Arm Chest Press
instructions:
1. Stand sideways to cable, hinge forward, and grab something for stability.
2. Press across the body going just short of extending your elbow.

exercise_name: Cable Braced Single Arm Internally Rotated Chest Fly
instructions:
1. Stand sideways to cable, hinge forward, and hold handle at shoulder height with an internally rotated shoulder.
2. Brace free hand against support, then bring working arm across chest in an arc.
3. Squeeze chest at the end, then slowly return to starting position.

exercise_name: Cable Kneeling High To Low Wood Chopper
instructions:

2. Rotate torso and pull handle diagonally down toward opposite hip.
3. Control the return, keeping core braced and hips stable.

2. Brace core and press handle straight forward without rotating torso.
3. Hold briefly, then return handle to chest under full control.

exercise_name: Cable Kneeling Pallof Press To Wood Chopper
instructions:
1. Kneel sideways to cable and press handle straight forward, keeping torso stable.
2. Rotate torso and pull handle diagonally across body into a wood chopper motion.
3. Return to center and bring handle back to chest under control.

1. Kneel sideways to cable and grip handle with both hands in front of chest.
2. Rotate torso and pull handle diagonally across body in a smooth arc.
3. Control the return, keeping core braced and hips stable.

1. Hold dumbbells at sides, hinge slightly, and explosively pull to shoulders.
2. Catch dumbbells at shoulder height with knees slightly bent.
3. Press overhead until arms extend, then lower under control.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Single Arm Clean And Press
instructions:

3. Press overhead until arm fully extends, then lower under control.

exercise_name: Dumbbell Single Arm Hang Clean And Press
instructions:

instructions:
1. Hold barbell behind you with arms at a 30 degree angle with your torso.
2. Shrug shoulders up and slightly back toward ears, keeping elbows locked.
3. Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control to full stretch.

3. Lower bar slowly behind head under control and repeat.

instructions:

2. Lower bar slowly toward upper chest or neck with elbows flared.
3. Press bar upward until arms fully extend without locking elbows.

3. Extend elbows to press bar back to starting position above chest.

instructions:

instructions:

3. Push through your heels and squat back up. Use the momentum you generate at the legs to push the bar over head.

exercise_name: Cable High Single Arm Hammer Bayesian Curl
instructions:
1. Stand facing away from high cable, hold handle with palm facing inward.
2. Step forward to create tension and let arm extend fully behind you.
3. Curl handle toward shoulder, keeping palm neutral, then lower slowly.

exercise_name: Cable High Single Arm Hammer Rolling Curl
instructions:
1. Set cable at about elbow level. Stand facing away from the cables and grab handle attachment.
2. Take a neutral grip, flex your elbows, as you curl hinge forward.
3. As you extend your elbow, also extend your hips until you are back in a standing position.

exercise_name: Cable High Single Arm Reverse Bayesian Curl
instructions:

2. Face away from the cable machine. Stagger your stance so you have a better base of support.
3. Face your palm downwards,  and curl.

exercise_name: Cable High Single Arm Reverse Rolling Curl
instructions:

2. Face away from the cable machine. Stagger your stance so you have a better base of support.
3. Face your palm downwards, as you curl hinge forward.
4. As you extend your elbow, also extend your hips until you are back in a standing position.

exercise_name: Cable High Single Arm Rolling Curl
instructions:

2. Take an underhand grip, flex your elbows, as you curl hinge forward.

4. As you press the kettlebells upwards, also push your hips forward to help generate additional power.
5. Slowly lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

exercise_name: Mini Band Plank Hand Step Out
instructions:
1. Loop band around wrists, hold high plank.
2. Step one hand outward against band tension.
3. Return and alternate, keeping hips level.

1. Loop band around ankles, hold high plank.
2. Step one foot outward, resisting band pull.
3. Return and alternate, maintaining stable hips.

1. Loop band around feet, start in high plank.
2. Drive one knee toward chest against resistance.
3. Alternate legs quickly while keeping hips stable.

1. Loop band around feet, start in high plank.
2. Step one foot forward under torso.
3. Alternate steps continuously, keeping hips stable.

1. Loop band around ankles, lie flat on back.
2. Raise both legs upward, keeping them straight.
3. Lower slowly without arching lower back.

1. Loop band around ankles, stand tall holding support.
2. Lift one leg forward with straight knee.
3. Lower slowly and repeat before switching sides.

1. Loop band above knees, lie on side knees bent.
2. Keep feet together, lift top knee upward, push bottom knee into ground, and push your hips forward.

exercise_name: Mini Band Side Laying Hip Abduction
instructions:
1. Loop band above knees, lie on side legs straight.
2. Lift top leg upward without rotating hips.
3. Lower slowly, maintaining constant band tension.

exercise_name: Mini Band Laying Glute Bridge Abduction
instructions:
1. Loop band above knees, lie on back knees bent.
2. Lift hips into bridge, hold top position.
3. Pulse knees outward against band, controlling tension.

1. Loop band above knees, lie on side legs straight.
2. Lift top leg upward without rotating hips.
3. Lower slowly, keeping tension on band.

1. Loop band above knees, lie on back knees bent.
2. Drive through heels, lift hips until fully extended.
3. Lower slowly, keeping knees pressed outward.

1. Loop band above knees, start on hands and knees.
2. Lift one knee outward, keeping hips level.
3. Lower slowly and repeat before switching sides.

1. Anchor band around foot, kneel tall holding ends.
2. Row elbow back, squeezing shoulder blades together.
3. Return slowly, maintaining upright torso.

1. Anchor band, elbow at side bent 90 degrees.
2. Rotate forearm outward, keeping elbow pinned.
3. Return slowly to start, controlling resistance.

1. Loop band above knees, stand feet shoulder width.
2. Squat down, knees pushed outward against band.
3. Explode upward into jump, land softly and reset.

target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, Lateral Hamstrings
instructions:
1. Stand on band with one foot, hinge at hips.
2. Keep back flat, opposite leg extended behind.
3. Drive hips forward to return upright.

exercise_name: Mini Band Alternating Bent Arm Lateral Raise
instructions:
1. Loop band around elbows, arms bent 90 degrees at sides.
2. Raise one elbow outward to shoulder height.
3. Lower slowly and alternate, keeping tension on band.

exercise_name: Mini Band Bent Arm Lateral Raise
instructions:
1. Loop band around elbows, arms bent 90 degrees at sides.
2. Raise both elbows outward to shoulder height.
3. Lower slowly and alternate, keeping tension on band.

1. Loop band above knees, stand shoulder width.
2. Sit back and down, pushing knees outward.
3. Stand up, maintaining tension on band.

1. Loop band above knees, slight athletic stance.
2. Step laterally, keeping knees pushed outward.
3. Continue stepping side to side under control.

exercise_name: Mini Band Walking Side To Side
instructions:
1. Loop band above knees, slight squat stance.
2. Step laterally, keeping constant outward knee pressure.
3. Continue walking side to side under control.

1. Loop band around wrists.
2. Keep one hand at shoulder height and press your other hand down.

1. Hold band at shoulder height, arms straight.
2. Pull hands apart, squeezing shoulder blades together.
3. Return slowly, maintaining tension throughout.

1. Loop bands around wrists,, arms extended overhead.
2. Pull arms outward in arc motion.
3. Return slowly, controlling tension throughout movement.

1. Loop band around feet or anchor behind you.
2. Lift one knee upward to hip height.
3. Lower slowly without leaning backward.

1. Loop band around ankles, stand tall holding support.
2. Extend one leg backward, squeezing glute.
3. Return slowly without arching lower back.

1. Loop band around ankles, stand tall holding support.
2. Lift one leg out to side, torso upright.
3. Lower slowly and repeat before switching sides.

1. Lay on an incline bench and hold the EZ bar with your elbows extended.
2. From this starting position, curl the bar up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body and your core engaged.
3. Slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

1. Lay on an incline bench and hold an incline bench with your elbows extended.
2. From this starting position, curl the bat up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body and your core engaged.
3. Slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

1. Stand inside trap bar, grip handles, stand tall.
2. Step one foot backward into lunge position.
3. Lower until front thigh is parallel to ground.
4. Push through front heel to return standing.

1. Take a large step forward with one leg, and lower your body until your back knee nearly touches the ground. Your front knee should be bent at a 90-degree angle, and your back leg should be straight.
2. Push through the heel of your front foot to raise your body back to the starting position.

target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, Lateral Hamstrings
instructions:
1. Stand inside trap bar, lift one foot slightly off ground.
2. Hinge hips back while lowering bar and extending free leg behind.
3. Keep hips square and back neutral throughout movement.
4. Drive through standing foot to return upright.

1. Stand inside trap bar, feet shoulder-width, grip handles.
2. Sit hips down and back, keeping chest upright.
3. Lower until thighs are at least parallel.
4. Push your heels into the floor and squat back to a standing position.

target_muscle: Glutes, Gluteus Maximus, Hamstrings, Lateral Hamstrings
instructions:
1. Stand inside trap bar. Place one foot behind. You should only be on your toes on that back foot. Your front foot should be planted flat the entire time.
2. Bend your back knee and push your hips back. Look for a stretch in your front hamstring.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward again until you're back in a standing position.

1. Stand on elevated surface inside trap bar, feet hip-width.
2. Grip handles, brace core, maintain neutral spine.
3. Drive through feet, extend hips and knees to stand.
4. Lower under control, maintaining balance on platform.

1. Set up inside trap bar with strong brace and neutral spine.
2. Lift bar slightly, pause just below knees.
3. Hold tension, then continue to full lockout.
4. Lower bar slowly back to floor.

1. Stand inside trap bar, arms straight, shoulders relaxed.
2. Elevate shoulders straight upward toward ears.
3. Pause briefly at top, squeezing traps.
4. Lower shoulders slowly to starting position.

1. Hold trap bar, stand tall with slight knee bend.
2. Hinge hips back, lowering bar along thighs.
3. Keep back neutral and tension in hamstrings.
4. Drive hips forward to return upright.

1. Hold EZ bar with overhand grip, elbows bent, bar at upper abdomen.
2. Raise elbows to shoulder height while externally rotating forearms upward.
3. Press bar overhead once forearms are vertical and aligned.
4. Reverse motion slowly back to starting position.

1. Be careful getting the bar into place overhead. Push your elbows forward while keeping your elbows tucked in.
2. Look for what feels like a stretch in your triceps. Extend your elbows until your fists are pointed at the ceiling.

1. Take a roughly shoulder width grip. There should be a straight line from your elbow to fist (vertical forearms).
2. Pull your chin back and press the weight toward the ceiling by extending at the elbow joint and flexing at the shoulder joint.
3. Press until your elbows are extended and push your head forward slightly.
4. Return to the start position with control. Pulling your chin back to allow the bar to pass your face safely.

1. Begin by standing with your feet about hip-width apart and holding the bar in the crook of your arms, near your belly. Your palms should be facing towards your body.
2. Sit back and lower your body down until your thighs are parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
3. Push through your heels to extend your hips and stand back up to the starting position.

1. Take a shoulder width, double overhand or mixed grip.
2. Push your hips back while leaving your knees mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
3. When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

1. Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart, with a barbell in front of you.
2. Bend down and grab the bar with an overhand grip, your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and your shoulders down as you grip the bar.
3. Stand up straight, lifting the bar off the ground and pulling it toward your hips. Keep your elbows close to your body and your shoulders down as you do this.
4. Once the bar is at your hips, raise your shoulders as high as you can, holding the position for a moment.

1. Take a double overhand roughly shoulder width grip.
2. Pull your elbows straight up the ceiling. Aim to get the bar to chin level or slightly higher.

1. Stand tall with an overhand grip on the barbell. Raise your arms until your fists are around eye level.
2. Return to the start under control. This exercise is prone to using momentum when you begin to fatigue.

target_muscle: Biceps, Long Head Bicep, Short Head Bicep
instructions:
1. Grab an EZ bar with an underhand grip.
2. Pull your elbows straight back and let the bar drag against your upper body (or very close to it).

1. Take a double overhand grip that's about shoulder width. Flex your elbows while keeping your elbows tucked in. Try not to let them flare out.
2. Curl until your forearm presses into your bicep. Then fully extend your elbows at the bottom of each rep.

1. Lie supine, knees bent, arms reaching toward thighs.
2. Lift head, shoulders, and upper back off carriage.
3. Reach forward while maintaining core engagement.
4. Lower slowly back to starting position.

target_muscle: Lower back, Abdominals, Glutes, Hamstrings, Quads
instructions:
1. Lie supine, feet in straps, legs extended upward.
2. Lift hips, rolling spine off carriage.
3. Bend knees, bringing heels toward glutes overhead.
4. Roll down slowly, extending legs to start.

1. Lie on reformer, heels together, toes apart on footbar.
2. Press carriage out by extending legs evenly.
3. Keep pelvis stable and core engaged.
4. Bend knees to return with control.

exercise_name: Pilates Single Arm Rotations Back Rowing Seated Row
instructions:
1. Sit tall, hold one strap, arm extended forward.
2. Pull handle while rotating torso slightly.
3. Keep hips stable and movement controlled.
4. Return arm and torso to start.

exercise_name: Pilates Single Arm Back Rowing Seated Row
instructions:
1. Sit tall, hold one strap, arm extended forward.
2. Pull handle toward torso, squeezing shoulder blade.
3. Keep torso stable without rotation.
4. Extend arm forward under control.

1. Sit tall, legs extended, hold straps with arms forward.
2. Pull handles toward torso, squeezing shoulder blades together.
3. Keep chest lifted and spine neutral throughout.
4. Extend arms forward under control.

1. Lie on reformer, one heel on footbar, other leg extended.
2. Press carriage out using working leg.
3. Keep hips level and core engaged.
4. Bend knee to return with control.

1. Stand beside reformer, one foot on carriage, one stable on floor.
2. Press carriage back by extending moving leg.

1. Lie prone, attach straps to ankles, legs extended.
2. Bend knees, pulling heels toward glutes.
3. Keep hips pressed down and core engaged.
4. Extend legs slowly back to start.
